title: chrysothamnus viscidiflorus var. puberulus
intro: Chrysothamnus viscidiflorus var. puberulus, also known as gray rabbitbrush, is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the aster family. It is native to the western United States and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and soothing digestive problems. Gray rabbitbrush can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory conditions.
